title: Bookshelf Hutch Storage Cabinet
summary: A frameless plywood bay-shelf cabinet — ripped plywood panels split into three bays by two verticals, with edge-banded exposed edges and no face frame.
description:
Frameless cabinetry skips the face frame entirely, so every plywood edge that would normally hide behind a frame rail is exposed instead and needs edge banding for a finished look. Rip your plywood panels to width on a table saw before crosscutting them to length — a straight, consistent rip is what keeps three bays reading as identical rather than subtly different widths.

Edge-band the long front edges of the top, verticals, and shelves before assembly, not after — it's far easier to run an iron down a flat, unobstructed edge than to work around adjoining panels once the cabinet is together. The 1x3 bay supports run along the underside of each shelf bay, tying the shelves to the back and adding rigidity across a span that would otherwise be plywood alone.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood panels to consistent width.

cutList.note: Rip to width, then crosscut to length.

steps.title: Rip and edge-band the panels
steps.body: Rip the plywood for the top, verticals, and shelves to 11-1/4" wide on the table saw, then crosscut each to length. Edge-band the exposed long front edge of each piece now, while it's still a flat, unobstructed panel — trim the banding flush once it's applied.

steps.title: Lay out and assemble the bays
steps.body: Position the two interior verticals to divide the cabinet into three equal bays. Drill pocket holes and attach the verticals between the top and where the bottom shelf will sit, with glue at every joint.

steps.title: Install the shelves
steps.body: Attach the shelves within each bay with pocket holes and glue, keeping all shelf heights consistent across the three bays.

steps.title: Add the bay supports
steps.body: Attach the 1x3 bay supports along the underside of the shelves, tying them to the back edge — this is what keeps a plywood shelf span from sagging over time.

steps.title: Attach the back and finish
steps.body: Nail the plywood back on with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, checking the whole cabinet for square right before it goes on. Fill any fastener holes, sand, and paint.

images.alt: A frameless three-bay plywood bookshelf hutch with edge-banded shelves
